ce:section.ce:para: This minimized fixture paraphrases the 2025 Remote Sensing of Environment article into a compact offline sample while preserving the section structure that the Elsevier XML parser relies on.
It focuses on seasonal vegetation greenness over Southeast Asia and keeps enough narrative body text to exercise heading extraction, section normalization and AI-markdown rendering.
The article compares geostationary observations with ancillary environmental signals to describe regional greenness cycles, cloud effects and temporal coverage tradeoffs.
Seasonal onset and decline vary across monsoon regimes, elevation and land cover, which makes high-cadence geostationary monitoring useful for resolving rapid transitions.
Stable vegetation-greenness timing helps downstream climate, agriculture and ecosystem analyses that need consistent phenology signals over large tropical regions.
